subject: Computer Networking
title: Self-Hosted vs Cloud Email
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Self-Hosted vs Cloud Email.

hook.text: Run your own email server or pay Google? The choice seems simple, but the trade-offs are deep.

buildup.text: Self-hosting gives full control: your data, your rules, your privacy. No third party reads your mail.

discovery.text: But maintaining deliverability alone is a full-time job. One misconfigured SPF record and you're blocklisted.

twist.text: Cloud providers handle spam filtering, uptime, and security patches. You trade control for convenience.

climax.text: Some run hybrid setups: cloud for delivery infrastructure, self-hosted for storage and encryption.

punchline.text: Self-host for control. Cloud for convenience. Hybrid for both.

quiz.question: What is the biggest challenge of self-hosting email?
options: The cost of electricity for the server
Finding an email client that supports self-hosted servers
